Hi Steph,

It was so nice speaking with you today, and I am thrilled you enjoyed your first session enough to lock in for the year.

I am happy to confirm that we have increased your session time to 50 minutes at a cost of $210 + GST per session. This will include 20 minutes for Junior Kindy and 30 minutes for Kindy. Please note that sessions will continue through school holidays.

I am also pleased to confirm you are all set with Coach Hannah, continuing fortnightly from your original start date. We are so glad to have you with us!
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.


Kind Regards,

. The under 2 age group children really like the basketball skills and loved the running with the ladders, but the lady seems o be overwhelmed when the children loose interest would be good if she can give simpler instructions

. The older children loved it some children that hadn’t participated in other weeks got in and gave it a go last week

 

I’m sorry if this wasn’t too descriptive but this is the feedback we have received so far

 

I hope this helps

Have a good day

Thank you

patterns.prevention:
• Map coach availability across all centres weekly and flag any gaps 48 hours before scheduled sessions to allow rescheduling or coverage swaps.

• Assign a primary and secondary coach to each centre to reduce inconsistency and build relationships with staff and families.

• Contact centres with merged programs or changing needs within 24 hours to confirm session continuation or adjust scheduling.

• Review coach utilisation rates to identify underutilised coaches who can backfill missed sessions or support struggling centres.

patterns.prevention:
• Audit coach engagement levels at all centres weekly—flag any coach scoring below 7/10 on energy/enthusiasm and pair with mentoring or reassignment within 5 days.

• Confirm backup coach availability before any centre starts and communicate succession plans to centre managers in writing at sign-up.

• Equip coaches with standardized activity plans and required equipment lists so programs stay challenging and consistent regardless of who's delivering.

• Schedule monthly check-ins with centre managers in weeks 2, 6, and 10 of each program to catch satisfaction issues early and solve them before cancellation.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres at 4-week and 8-week milestones to confirm satisfaction and identify issues early before cancellation decisions are made.

• Schedule quarterly check-ins with centre directors to discuss program value, pricing concerns, and competitive threats from other providers.

• Document all cancellation reasons in a shared tracker and flag "switching provider" patterns monthly so teams can proactively address at-risk accounts.

• Review pricing and contract terms for centres with budget concerns noted in cancellations, and prepare alternative package options to present before they source alternatives.

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Ultimately, I believe that if we had a higher-level coach available to replace Brendan immediately when he quit, the center would likely still be running sessions with us.

  - Jan 19: Liz emailed to cancel the service due to head office budget cuts. I confirmed the four-week notice period, setting the final week for February 19th.
  - Feb 13–16: Efforts were made to save the account by offering different session types (music). I spoke with Sophie Jones to provide the formal four-session notice information.

March 2026: Scheduling Conflicts and Billing Disputes

  - Mar 5–9: Coach Noah reported significant issues; the center was unaware of his arrival, and the 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM slot clashed with nap times. I suggested moving the remaining sessions to a morning slot (8:30 AM – 11:30 AM).
  - Mar 12–18: Sophie Jones (Senior Manager) stated they could not accommodate the new times due to other bookings. She questioned if the final four weeks had been paid for or required an invoice.
  - Mar 23: The center cancelled the final session with Noah, citing the unsuitable time and their existing bookings.

Next point of contact:
Dollow up term 4
signals: AI reasoning: Centre cancelled due to budget, then booked other incursions on those timeslots instead. They switched providers/programs.
